input.headline: Working from home in sectional title units: Know the rules to avoid eviction

raw.headline: Working from home in sectional title units: Know the rules to avoid eviction

winner: Why did the WFH employee bring a ladder to the communal garden? To *section* the unit into "work from home" and "eviction zones.

candidates.text: My boss told me I could work from home forever—turns out he meant *his* couch, not mine. Eviction letter arrived today with a coffee stain.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with coffee stain adds a specific, visual punch; concise and funny without being generic."}

candidates.text: Why did the remote worker get evicted? Because they kept saying, "I'm just sectioning out my time!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on 'sectional title units' and 'sectioning out my time,' offering surprise and specificity. However, it's slightly forced and could be more concise for maximum impact."}

candidates.text: Why did the WFH employee bring a ladder to the communal garden? To *section* the unit into "work from home" and "eviction zones.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on 'section' and the context of sectional title units. It's specific to the topic and has a surprising twist, though the explanation feels slightly forced."}

candidates.text: So my neighbor's trying to get me kicked out for working from home in our sectional title unit. Said I was "cluttering the common areas" with my desk. I said, "Sir, I'm just a home office professional. If you see this as clutter, then maybe it's time to declutter your life." He didn't laugh—just filed a complaint and now I'm getting letters about "unapproved use of space." Classic.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is specific and situational with a good punchline, but the humor is somewhat mild and relies on a relatable scenario rather than a surprising twist."}

candidates.text: My neighbor’s idea of a "team meeting" is blasting music and expecting me to take notes on a Post-it I can't reach from my bed. Eviction notice delivered in crayon.
